summary: Set 093 Session 1: every Work Explorer module row now renders two PERSISTENT semantic child nodes — Plan (present/missing) and Session sets (blocked-until-plan / empty / bucketed, with the status buckets nested UNDER Session sets, never replacing the checklist). ARIA levels shifted to module 1 / Plan & Session sets 2 / bucket 3 / row 4 (aria-setsize=2 on the fixed pair; aria-expanded on Session sets only when bucketed); keyboard nav and collapse reach the new nodes via the existing shared toggler. States derive from the pure deriveModuleChildren(planPresent, setCount); planExists is resolved host-side per root (guarded against a null planPath) and ORed across worktree roots in mergeVisibleModules, with the null guard also enforced in the pure buildVisibleModulePayloads so a fallback (planPath null) can never read present. A routed architecture ruling (opus, tier 3) settled that all three module kinds render both children with no exemption. Layer 2 (deriveModuleChildren matrix, per-kind payload, merge-OR) and Layer 3 (session-sets-tree + module-tier) pins moved with the new level (amendment 6). Full suite re-run GREEN against the final remediated tree (postdates R3–R5): pytest 2922 passed/6 skipped, extension unit 1420 passing, Playwright Layer 3 23 passed, tsc clean. No Marketplace publish (single release boundary after Set 094). Verification: R1 Major (host-only null guard) fixed; R2 VERIFIED; the close backstop (R3) then surfaced a distinct never-hide-work Major — the optional sessionSets field degraded to a leaf that dropped buckets for a fields-less legacy payload — fixed by rendering bucketed whenever any bucket carries rows; R4 refined it (guard must consult rows.length as well as count) and its root, so the class was closed STRUCTURALLY: plan/sessionSets are now REQUIRED on ModulePayload (both producers emit them — the test-only legacy buildModulePayloads updated), eliminating the field-less-payload vector at the type level, with the webview count||rows guard kept as defense in depth. R5 closed the class at the TERMINAL gate: renderBucket now decides emptiness from bucket.rows (not the display count), so no row-rendering path can drop populated rows on a stale-count payload — count===rows.length is invariant in the sole producer buildBucketPayloads, so this is defense in depth. client.js is covered by the documented source-scan house pattern (IIFE, not unit-importable) plus Layer 3.

reason.specifics: Session 2 (row action strip, QuickPick retirement, assign-legacy flow) builds directly on Session 1's child-node model and touches the same host/webview/protocol surfaces; the routed analysis (gemini-2.5-pro, s1-assignment-analysis.json) recommends the same Opus seat at medium effort because the warm architectural context sharply lowers the cost of the cross-cutting pickModuleForAuthoring targeting-seam refactor and the set-terminal path-aware critique.
